Suppose $T : \mathscr{C} \to \mathscr{C}$ is an idempotent endofunctor, with $\mu : T^2 \xrightarrow{\;\cong\;} T$. An expansion of $T$ is an object $A \in \mathscr{C}$ together with a morphism $a : A \to TA$ such that $\mu \circ Ta = 1$. Expansions of $T$ form a category, $\mathsf{Exp}(T)$, which is a full subcategory of $T$-coalgebras. Denote the forgetful functor as follows.
$$
U : \mathsf{Exp}(T) \to \mathscr{C}
$$
***lemma.*** Suppose $\mathscr{C}$ has a zero object, and  $T0 = 0$. Then $\mathsf{Exp}(T)$ has a zero object.
***proof.*** $0 : 0 \to 0$ is an expansion and is the zero object.

***lemma.*** Again suppose $T$ preservers zero. Suppose $f : A \to B$ is a morphism in $\mathsf{Exp}(T)$ such that kernel of $Uf$ exists (in $\mathscr{C}$) and is preserved by $T$ in the sense the following diagram commutes. It follows that $\ker f$ exists (in $\mathsf{Exp}(T)$) and is preserved by $U$. The result also holds for cokernels.
<iframe class="quiver-embed" src="https://q.uiver.app/#q=WzAsMyxbMiwwLCJcXGtlciBUIGYiXSxbMSwyLCJUQSJdLFswLDAsIlQgXFxrZXIgZiJdLFswLDEsIlxca2VyIFRmIiwwLHsibGFiZWxfcG9zaXRpb24iOjQwfV0sWzIsMSwiVFxca2VyIGYiLDIseyJsYWJlbF9wb3NpdGlvbiI6NDB9XSxbMiwwLCJcXGNvbmciLDAseyJzdHlsZSI6eyJ0YWlsIjp7Im5hbWUiOiJhcnJvd2hlYWQifX19XV0=&embed" width="700" height="200" style="border-radius: 8px; border: none; filter: invert(1);"></iframe>
***proof.*** Observe. The left diagram defines the expansion kernel $k \in \mathsf{Exp}(T)$, and the right diagram proves that it is actually an expansion ($\mu \circ Tk = 1$).
<iframe class="quiver-embed" src="https://q.uiver.app/#&embed" width="700" height="300" style="border-radius: 8px; border: none; filter: invert(1);"></iframe>
